text:
Once upon a time, in a forgotten cabin nestled deep in the woods, there lived a humble traveler. Every day he would rise early, weave his own clothes from strips of bark, and set off into the forest. He had no map, no companion; just determination and curiosity.

One serene dawn, he stumbled upon a clearing. There, in the heart of the green, sat an old oak tree. The traveler spoke to it, "Old friend, tell me your secrets."

The tree tilted its wise head. "Ah, young one, few bother. I've witnessed countless travelers come and go, leaving but tales of your folly."

His heart brimmed with excitement. "Please, share a secret?"

"Look around." The tree said. "There's no one here!"

The traveler glanced around, heart pounding. The woods were empty. "I'd swear someone was right behind me!"

"Sometimes," the tree murmured softly, "you see what you want."

The traveler
